premise: When I was 57, my daughter forged my signature on a reverse mortgage and drained $190,000 in equity from my paid-off home, leaving me with a foreclosure notice while she bought a condo out of state; I proved the identity theft, sued for fraud, recovered the deed plus $220,000 in damages, and now she’s my live-in caregiver on court order, paying back every cent with interest at the same bank where she got the loan.
